product.name: ArNeo Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Fluorescencyjne dzianiny hi-vis na odzież ostrzegawczą
product.familyDescriptorEn: Fluorescent hi-vis knits for safety wear
product.categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
product.titleDescriptor: Flame Retardant, High Visible, Safety Jacket Fabric
product.specLine: 150 g/m², 100% Polyester, High Visible, Flame Retardant Knit Fabric
descriptions: ArNeo Pro is 100% polyester, 150 g/m² warp knitted flame retardant neon colored fabric for safety jackets.
It is mostly used by aeroport apron workers.
Neon dyed fabrics they attract more attention than other colors. They absorb uv light and reflect as a visible light.
As a result they shine more than other objects.
Flame retardant certified. (TS EN ISO 14116) (resistant to 10 washing)
Flame Retardant, High Visible, Safety Jacket Fabric
150 g/m², 100% Polyester, High Visible, Flame Retardant Knit Fabric
technicalParameters: Dimensional Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicitly certified to EN ISO 20471 and marketed as a fluorescent knit fabric for high-visibility safety jackets.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Description mentions aeroport apron workers, but this is an occupational context, not explicit vehicle/vessel transport application.

evidence: applicationImageLabels: Yellow/Orange/Red Flame Retardant Safety Jacket EN 20471
primary.rationale: Explicitly certified to EN ISO 20471 and marketed as a fluorescent knit fabric for high-visibility safety jackets, which is the fabric's clearest and most central commercial use.

secondary.rationale: The mention of airport apron workers describes an occupational context, not an explicit vehicle/vessel transport application, so no independently supported secondary application exists.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Aeroport apron worker context is occupational, not an explicit vehicle/vessel transport use as required by the taxonomy.
A more specific certified hi-vis safety jacket application dominates over generic workwear classification.
